Petition To Sell Personal Property Pursuant To Probate Code Section 2541
Off Calendar Mar-16-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Thomas Lucas. Court Appointed counsel Trisha Friedeberg for (conservatee) Trudy Sachs.

The Court stated that the issue is the statutory requirement regarding the sale of personal property, that either a statement of consent or evidence of lack of capacity is needed, that we now have a declaration regarding a neuropsych filing and asked if there is any other information. Ms. Alavi reported that Mr. Lucas has attempted to get an updated capacity declaration from the doctor but has not heard back, that they filed a supplement, that the sale needs to take place to keep her benefits and referenced a neuropsych report from 2013. Ms. Friedeberg reported that she met with Ms. Sachs, that Ms. Sachs still recalls the jewelry, that she had no recollection of the gold coins or platinum bar but once told about them she said not to sell them, that Ms. Friedeberg does not believe she has capacity to exercise consent, that she was not appointed in 2013 but in reading the capacity declaration it seems consistent with the interaction with Ms. Sachs now. Ms. Alavi stated that she is not sure that Ms. Sachs needs to be examined, that they have followed up with the doctor but are unable to get the completed capacity declaration and that Ms. Sachs assets are dwindling and they need to apply for the benefits. The Court granted the petition to sell personal property pursuant to probate code section 2541 (subject to review of the order) based on the evidence before the court and only for the non jewelry items.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos & Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition To Sell Personal Property Pursuant To Probate Code Section 2541
Off Calendar Feb-23-2016 Continued To Mar-15-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Sell Personal Property Pursuant To Probate Code Section 2541
Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Continued To Feb-23-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Thomas Lucas. Court appointed counsel Trisha Friedeberg for (conservatee) Trudy Sachs.

The Court stated that it may need to continue this matter and that the petition does not satisfy P.C. 2545(c)(2) regarding capacity. Ms. Friedeberg stated that Ms. Sachs has no recollection of the gold coins or platinum bar, that she does remember and would like to keep a ring that belonged to her mother and that she does not remember the other items or object to selling them. The Court asked if we need something stating that she has/lacks capacity. Ms. Friedeberg stated that Ms. Sachs distinctly remembered the ring, that she has dementia and that she feels Ms. Sachs would have voiced concerns regarding the other items if she had any. Ms. Alavi stated that she feels Ms. Sachs lacks capacity and that she can have Mr. Lucas visit her. The Court stated that it is a statutory requirement and that we need to know if she is able to exercise her desires. Ms. Alavi stated that Ms. Friedeberg could go back with the specific items and see if she remembers them. The Court stated that she either consents or lacks capacity. Ms. Friedeberg stated that they can have a doctor evaluate her. Ms. Alavi stated that there is an 18k gold ring and an emerald that Ms. Sachs would like to keep. The Court stated that it will consider the request withdrawn regarding these items. Ms. Friedeberg stated that Ms. Sachs is interested in moving to the Jewish Home and that as a Holocaust survivor she will have priority. The petition to sell personal property was continued to February 23, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
